page: Microsoft Azure F16as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Fasv7 family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The processor configuration includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable and operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression registers in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average range. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks, along with PassMark database operations and Redis SET operations, also perform within the middle 50% tier.

Due to its high single-core clock speed of 4.5 GHz and strong single-threaded performance, the F16as_v7 is suited for workloads that depend heavily on single-thread execution speed, such as lightweight LLM text generation using small models, which achieves top-tier token generation speeds. However, the lack of local storage and GPU acceleration, combined with average multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics, represents a tradeoff for highly parallelized or data-intensive database tasks. It is best utilized for general-purpose applications, web serving, and single-threaded processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure F16as_v7 is an x86_64 server in the Fasv7 family, featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz and 64.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this virtualized instance relies on Premium Storage compatibility. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core, single-threaded gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 10%. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations align with average tiers. The server is suited for workloads requiring high single-thread execution speeds, such as lightweight language model inference, web serving, and general-purpose compute tasks that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16as_v7 is a Fasv7 family server with 16 AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs at 4.5 GHz and 64 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier single-core performance but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F16as_v7 is a Fasv7 server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance. It lacks local storage.
tagline: An AMD-powered server featuring high single-core clock speeds and balanced memory allocation for single-threaded and general-purpose workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for web serving, lightweight LLM inference, and single-threaded tasks
